parts.answer:  decreasing AND to at least 1 °C x3  x2  x1 e.g. ‘cooling rate falls steadily as time goes on’ AND reference to cooling rate values to show this  F near or equal to  R (e.g.) x2 lower than x1 and second 90 s has lower starting temperature 1 • volume of water • size / material of beaker • room temperature / other valid environmental factor May/June 2025 Question Answer Marks V  3.0 V and I  1.00 A I all decreasing RA RB and RC within 10% of each other 1 within limits of experimental accuracy owtte AND supported by values from table 1 May/June 2025 Question Answer Marks 4 MP1 apparatus: protractor ruler MP2 method: shine ray from ray-lamp into the block trace ray emerging from block measure angle of refracted ray MP3 repeat for new concentration 1 MP4 control variable: angle of ray from ray lamp MP5 table: columns, with units, for concentration and dependent variable any variable mentioned must have correct units MP6 analysis: compare readings in the table to see if change in concentration produces change in dependent variable plot line graph (with axes specified) MP7 additional point: any one from: • at least 5 sets of data taken • repeat for each value of independent variable AND take average
